title: trifolium bivestitum
intro: Trifolium bivestitum, also known as the two-veined clover, is a species of clover that belongs to the legume family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Trifolium bivestitum include its potential to reduce symptoms of menopause and other hormonal imbalances, as well as its ability to act as an anti-diarrheal agent. Trifolium bivestitum can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in infusions or dried for later use.
